chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Cr. Bail Appl. No.37 of 2025 Cr. Bail Appl. No.2725 of 2024 16.1.2025 Mr. Tariq Hussain, advocate for applicant in Cr. B.A.No.37/2025 Mr. Farooqullah, advocate for applicant in Cr. B.A.No.2725/2024 Mr. Athar Hussain, advocate for complainant. Mr. Abrar Ali Khichi, Addl.P.G. = O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: On 15.08.2023 when complainant alongwith his uncle Kamran was going on a motorcycle, at Library Traffic Signal Sun-set Boulevard Road Karachi, he was waylaid by four unknown accused, who robbed Rs.500,000/- from him, hence FIR. During investigation applicant Abdul Rehman and Muhammad Amir were arrested on 27.09.2023 and from them respectively Rs.35000/- and 25000/- of robbed money were recovered. Whereas, applicant Akhtar @ Papoo was arrested on 05.10.2023 and from him Rs.,25000/- were recovered. 2. It is stated by the learned counsel in defence that co-accused Muhammad Amir has been granted bail in Cr. B.A.No.969 of 2024 by this court vide order dated 21.10.2024 and case of the applicant is on identical footing, hence rule of consistency is applicable. 3. Learned Addl.P.G.has opposed bail so also learned counsel for the complainant. However in this case rule of consistency appears to be applicable as co- accused Muhammad Amir having been assigned similar role has been granted bail by this Court already. Accordingly, these applications are allowed and applicants are granted bail subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The observations made herein above are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. 5.

Accordingly, these applications are allowed and applicants are granted bail subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The observations made herein above are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. 5. These two Cr. Bail Applications are disposed of. J U D G E Hafiz
